question: What is the notice period for terminating a permanent worker under Section 27?
expected_answer_summary: A permanent worker must give 60 days notice in writing to terminate employment.

question: What is the notice period required for an employer to terminate a permanent worker under Section 26?
expected_answer_summary: An employer must give 120 days notice for monthly rated workers or 60 days notice for other workers.

question: What are maximum daily working hours for an adult worker under Section 100?
expected_answer_summary: No adult worker shall ordinarily be required or allowed to work in an establishment for more than 8 hours in any day.

question: How is overtime payment calculated under Section 108?
expected_answer_summary: Overtime work entitles a worker to allowance at the rate of twice his ordinary rate of basic wage.

question: How many days of casual leave is a worker entitled to in a calendar year under Section 115?
expected_answer_summary: Every worker shall be entitled to casual leave with full wages for 10 days in a calendar year.

question: How is annual leave with wages calculated for adult workers in a factory under Section 117?
expected_answer_summary: Every adult worker who has completed one year of continuous service is entitled to annual leave at the rate of one day for every 18 days of work.

question: Within how many days after the expiry of the wage period must wages be paid under Section 120?
expected_answer_summary: Wages of a worker shall be paid before the expiry of the seventh day after the last day of the wage period.

question: What are permissible deductions from wages under Section 125?
expected_answer_summary: Deductions may be made for fines, absence from duty, damage to goods, accommodation, or provident fund contributions.

question: What is the procedure for an aggrieved worker to submit a written complaint to the employer under Section 33?
expected_answer_summary: The worker must submit the grievance in writing to the employer within 30 days of the cause of grievance.

question: Who may raise an industrial dispute under Section 209?
expected_answer_summary: An industrial dispute may be raised by an employer or a collective bargaining agent.
